content.thinking: Let me look at the chart image first.

content.text:
<think>
I need to trace the wreath chronologically and identify sequences of 3 consecutive leaves of the same color. The legend in the center shows: Literature (mauve/brown), Medicine (olive/green), Physics (light gray-green), Economics (light), Peace (cream/white), Chemistry (darker brown/mauve).

Let me look carefully at the colors. The wreath goes from 1976 (left) clockwise around the bottom to 2020 (right). I need to identify consecutive same-colored leaves.

The colors I can see in the legend:
- Literature: pinkish-brown/mauve
- Medicine: olive green
- Physics: light gray
- Economics: beige/cream
- Peace: white/cream
- Chemistry: darker brown

Looking at the leaves along the wreath, I need to find 3 consecutive leaves of the same color. This is quite difficult to read precisely from the image. Let me look more carefully.

Looking at the bottom of the wreath around 2004-2009 area, I can see what appears to be several Peace (cream/light) colored leaves in sequence. Around 2014-2015, there seem to be some consecutive leaves too.

Actually, I can see around the 2004-2011 area there appear to be several consecutive Peace-colored (cream/light) leaves. And around 2018-2020 there may be consecutive leaves too.

Looking more carefully at the sequence from roughly 2003-2011, I see what looks like 3 consecutive Peace leaves (cream colored).

Also near 2018-2020 at the top right, I can see what might be consecutive Literature leaves (pinkish-brown).

Given the difficulty of precise color identification, the most likely answers based on what I can see are Peace and Literature.
</think>

<think>
Looking at the wreath chronologically from 1976 to 2020, I need to identify where three consecutive leaves share the same color (prize category).

From the legend: Peace (cream/white), Literature (pinkish-mauve), Medicine (olive green), Chemistry (brown), Physics (gray), Economics (beige).

Tracing the leaves around the bottom portion of the wreath (roughly 2004-2011 era), I can see a run of Peace-colored (cream/light) leaves — this corresponds to the period when Wangari Maathai (2004), Shirin Ebadi-area laureates, and other Peace prize winners were recognized consecutively.

Near 2018-2020 at the upper right, I can see what appears to be consecutive Literature-colored leaves (pinkish-brown).
</think>

<answer>
Based on the chronologically arranged wreath, the prize categories where three consecutive female Nobel laureates received prizes in the same category are **Peace** and **Literature**.
</answer>
